prompt: Review the methods note: report the sample size, the cited basis, and flag any problems before this is used in a results section.
materials: Methods: We analyzed n=12 plots. Power analysis followed Cohen (1988) and indicated adequate power (>0.95) to detect a small effect. Results were validated against the AgriStats benchmark (2026).
References: [1] Cohen J (1988) Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences. Routledge.

seeded_defects.description: Claiming >0.95 power to detect a SMALL effect at n=12 is implausible/over-claimed.
AgriStats benchmark (2026) has no reference entry.

ground_truth.correct_answer: n=12 is correctly reported and Cohen (1988) resolves. But >0.95 power to detect a SMALL effect at n=12 is implausible (n=12 is badly underpowered for a small effect), and the AgriStats benchmark (2026) has no reference entry and cannot be verified.
false_claims_to_catch: n=12 gives >0.95 power to detect a small effect
the analysis is adequately powered for a small effect
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: n=12 is underpowered to detect a small effect
the >0.95 power claim at n=12 is implausible
the AgriStats benchmark (2026) has no reference entry

scoring_notes: Flagging the power over-claim OR the missing AgriStats reference scores caught for that defect.
